title: Recorded Statement — Insured (Maria Chen)

metadata.consent_capture_text: I, Maria Chen, consent to this conversation being recorded for the purposes of investigating claim HO-2026-04-04217.

preamble: This is a recorded statement taken in connection with claim HO-2026-04-04217. The insured has acknowledged the recording. Statements provided here are made under the policy's cooperation clause.

transcript.speaker: Adjuster
transcript.text: Maria, thanks for taking this call. As we discussed, this conversation is being recorded. Can you confirm you consent to being recorded?

transcript.speaker: Insured
transcript.text: Yes, I consent.

transcript.speaker: Adjuster
transcript.text: Please state your full name and address for the record.

transcript.speaker: Insured
transcript.text: Maria Chen, 4521 Oak Ridge Drive, Plano, Texas, 75024.

transcript.speaker: Adjuster
transcript.text: Thank you. I'd like to walk through the day of the loss. Can you describe what happened?

transcript.speaker: Insured
transcript.text: Sure. I had been at my sister's place in Frisco that afternoon. I came home and saw the damage. The roof looked terrible — shingles down in the yard, the skylight was cracked. I called my husband first, then called you all the next morning.

transcript.speaker: Adjuster
transcript.text: So just to clarify — you weren't at the property when the hailstorm occurred?

transcript.speaker: Insured
transcript.text: No, I wasn't there during the storm. I came home afterwards. I think I got back around six in the evening.

transcript.speaker: Adjuster
transcript.text: And what date was that?

transcript.speaker: Insured
transcript.text: It was on the 22nd. The day I called in.

transcript.speaker: Adjuster
transcript.text: OK. Was anyone home during the storm?

transcript.speaker: Insured
transcript.text: No, the house was empty. My husband was at work and my son was at school.

transcript.speaker: Adjuster
transcript.text: Can you describe the damage as you found it?

transcript.speaker: Insured
transcript.text: The south side of the roof was the worst — I could see chunks of shingles on the lawn. The skylight in the kitchen was cracked through. There was already water on the kitchen counter from where it had dripped. The front gutter was bent up.

transcript.speaker: Adjuster
transcript.text: Was any mitigation done after you discovered the damage?

transcript.speaker: Insured
transcript.text: Yes. We had a roofer named Lone Star come out the next morning and tarp the skylight.

transcript.speaker: Adjuster
transcript.text: Do you have a receipt for that mitigation work?

transcript.speaker: Insured
transcript.text: I think it was paid by credit card. I'll have to check. I don't think I have a paper receipt yet.

transcript.speaker: Adjuster
transcript.text: OK, please send anything you can find. Last question — is the contractor estimate from Lone Star Premier Roofing the only estimate you've obtained?

transcript.speaker: Insured
transcript.text: Yes, just the one.

transcript.speaker: Adjuster
transcript.text: Understood. That concludes my questions for today. Thank you for your cooperation.

transcript.speaker: Insured
transcript.text: Thank you.
